title: Auth0 Identity Provider
description: Auth0 as a managed OIDC/OAuth2 identity provider. Handles social login (30+ providers), enterprise federation via Active Directory and LDAP, machine-to-machine credentials, customizable login flows via Actions, and MFA. Well-suited to B2C and B2B apps that need flexible social and enterprise login without operating identity infrastructure.

cost.costNotes: Free tier: 7,500 MAU, unlimited logins, social connections, MFA. Essentials plan from $23/month for 1,000 MAU. Professional from $240/month with advanced enterprise features and SLA.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Auth0 Free tier supports 7,500 MAU at $0/month with no credit card required. Essentials starts at $23/month for 1,000 MAU with $0.023/MAU overage. Min=0 reflects free tier; max=23 reflects entry paid tier for small apps.
provenance.adoptionCost: Auth0 has SDKs for all major languages and quickstart guides. Integration: create application in dashboard, install SDK, configure callback URLs, add login button (~2 hours at $50/hour = $100). AI tools reduce boilerplate significantly.
provenance.licenseCost: Auth0 Free tier requires no license fees. Commercial use allowed on free tier up to 7,500 MAU.

provides.reasoning: Auth0 is a purpose-built managed identity provider service delivering full OAuth2/OIDC and SAML identity services.
Auth0 handles username/password, social, enterprise, and passwordless authentication flows.
Auth0 provides SSO across multiple applications via shared session and Universal Login.
Auth0 supports 30+ social identity providers including Google, GitHub, Apple, Facebook.
Auth0 supports MFA via TOTP (Google Authenticator), SMS, push notifications, and WebAuthn.
Auth0 supports M2M authentication via OAuth2 client credentials flow for API-to-API auth.

requires.reasoning: Auth0 is a cloud SaaS requiring internet access for all authentication flows.

availability.reason: Auth0 is publicly available with a generous free tier

supports_nfr.reason: Auth0 is an OAuth2/OIDC identity provider. Specs using other auth mechanisms (mtls, api_key, jwt, saml) do not require an external IdP of this type.
Auth0 guarantees 99.99% uptime SLA on Professional and Enterprise plans; free/Essentials tiers have best-effort availability without contractual SLA.
Auth0 Universal Login and token endpoints introduce minimum 100ms p95 latency due to hosted login page round-trips and token issuance.
Auth0 p99 latency floor is ~300ms due to geographic routing variability and peak-load token issuance.
Auth0 is a managed SaaS; recovery from outages depends on Auth0 infrastructure, not application-level rollback.
Auth0 HIPAA BAA is only available on Enterprise plan. Free and Essentials tiers must not process PHI.
Auth0 is GDPR-compliant with data processing agreements available; applicable to all paid tiers and free tier with configuration.
Auth0 supports CCPA compliance through data export and deletion APIs available on all tiers.
Auth0 SOC 2 Type II certification supports SOX audit trail requirements on Professional and Enterprise plans.

supports_constraints.reason: Auth0 is a cloud-agnostic SaaS identity provider that works with any cloud or on-premises deployment.
Auth0 provides official SDKs for all major languages and works with any backend via standard OAuth2/OIDC HTTP flows.
Auth0 supports web apps (Universal Login), mobile (native SDKs), and API authorization (JWT/M2M).
Pattern requires Auth0 as the identity provider.
Auth0 Organizations feature supports multi-tenant identity management for B2B SaaS.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to caching requirements.

connection_type.description: Login UI mode. universal-login (hosted by Auth0, recommended — protects against phishing), embedded (custom UI using Auth0 APIs — requires careful XSS mitigation), social-only, or enterprise-only.

mfa_enabled.description: Enable multi-factor authentication for all users. Recommended for any app with sensitive data. Can be enforced via Auth0 policies or selectively via Rules/Actions.

social_connections.description: Social identity providers to enable (e.g., google-oauth2, github, apple). Each requires configuring OAuth2 app credentials in the respective provider's developer console.

token_lifetime_seconds.description: Access token lifetime in seconds. Default 86400 (24h). Shorter lifetimes improve security; longer lifetimes reduce re-authentication friction. Refresh token rotation recommended for SPAs and mobile.

warn_nfr.reason: Availability above 99.9% requires Auth0 Professional or Enterprise plan with contractual SLA.
warn_nfr.message: idp-oidc--auth0: availability target {actual} exceeds free/Essentials tier best-effort availability. Upgrade to Professional (99.99% SLA) or implement fallback authentication for high-availability requirements.
